Professor View 01 / 40
General English -- 144 Aulas

Rubens Tofolo

From conference lapses back to full fluency -- in 144 lessons

B1- Belém, Pará Endocrinologist 60 min 2x/week
Progresso Geral0%
Velocidade:
Rubens Tofolo
55 anos
Belém, Pará
Endocrinologista, dono de clínica
B1- (A2+ em produção)
General English -- Fluência Conversacional
2x por semana, 60 min
144 aulas de 60 min

Jornada de Transformação

De: Um especialista que domina a área, mas perde compreensão nos congressos internacionais e sente que o inglês "enferrujou" desde a Califórnia

Para: O médico que participa de congressos em inglês com compreensão plena, faz networking com naturalidade e apresenta pesquisas com confiança

Promessa do Programa

"De 'aquele lapso no congresso' de volta ao médico que dava palestras em inglês na Califórnia, em 144 aulas."

Forças

  • Exposição prévia intensa: morou na Califórnia (1994-96)
  • Poliglota: fala 7 idiomas, excelente aptidão linguística
  • Vocabulário técnico médico já internalizado
  • Motivação clara: congressos e viagens internacionais
  • Vivência cultural ampla: 95 países visitados

Pontos de Melhoria

  • Fluência oral "enferrujada" por falta de prática diária
  • Dificuldade de compreensão em velocidade nativa
  • Confusão entre tempos verbais (present simple vs present perfect)
  • Produção abaixo do nível receptivo (A2+ vs B1-)
  • Vocabulário cotidiano limitado fora do contexto médico

Currículo -- 144 Aulas

Metodologia

PPP Model

Presentation, Practice, Production. Cada aula segue essa sequência: apresentação do conteúdo, prática controlada, produção livre.

Communicative Approach

Foco em comunicação real. Rubens pratica situações que enfrenta em congressos, clínica e viagens.

Task-Based Learning

Cada aula tem uma tarefa concreta: apresentar-se num congresso, explicar um caso clínico, negociar.

Diagnostic Assessment

Aula 1 mapeia o nível real de produção. Ajustes contínuos baseados em performance.

Spaced Repetition

Vocabulário revisitado em intervalos crescentes. Callback diário das últimas 3 aulas no warm-up.

Perfil de Personalidade

Alta - comunicativo, gosta de compartilhar histórias
Muito alta - 95 países, 7 idiomas
Moderada - prefere acertar, mas aceita correção
Alta - quer voltar a apresentar em congressos
Alta - empresário, acostumado a liderar
Moderado - detalhista, gosta de entender a fundo
Direto mas diplomático - profissional experiente

Welcome, Rubens!

Onboarding

Welcome to the program! Before each lesson, complete the Pre-class activities. Here are 5 emergency phrases you can use at any time:

1 Could you repeat that, please?
2 I specialize in endocrinology and obesity.
3 I have been practicing medicine for over twenty-five years.
4 Could you speak a little slower, please?
5 It is nice to meet you. I am Dr. Tofolo from Brazil.
Aula 01 -- Pre-class

Who Is Rubens Today

Diagnostic Conversation and Reactivation Baseline
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Learn the key words for this lesson. Click Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Specialize -- to focus on one particular area of expertise
"I specialize in endocrinology and obesity management."
Background -- your education, training, and professional experience
"His background includes research in the United States and France."
Currently -- at the present time, right now
"I am currently leading a study on metabolic syndrome."
Fluency -- the ability to speak a language smoothly and naturally
"She achieved fluency in English after living abroad."
Conference -- a large formal meeting for professional discussion
"The conference attracted specialists from forty countries."
Recall -- to remember something from the past
"I can recall presenting research in California in nineteen ninety-five."
Comprehensive -- complete, including all necessary details
"We need a comprehensive review of the latest data."
Reactivate -- to make something active or working again
"He wants to reactivate his English for international events."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word to its correct definition.

Specialize
Background
Currently
Fluency
Conference
Recall
Comprehensive
Reactivate

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text below and answer the questions.

Dr. Rubens Tofolo specializes in endocrinology. He currently runs a clinic in Belem, Brazil. His background includes a PhD from France, and he has visited ninety-five countries. He recalls his years in California with great fondness. He has been practicing medicine for over twenty-five years. Now, he wants to reactivate his English fluency so he can participate in international conferences with comprehensive understanding.
1. Which sentence uses the Present Perfect tense?
A "He currently runs a clinic in Belem."
B "He has visited ninety-five countries."
C "He wants to reactivate his English fluency."
2. Why does the text say "specializes" (Present Simple) instead of "has specialized"?
A Because he specialized only in the past
B Because it is a permanent fact about his profession
C Because he just started specializing
3. "He has been practicing medicine for over twenty-five years." What does this tell us?
A He stopped practicing medicine
B He practiced for exactly twenty-five years
C He started in the past and still practices now

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Present Simple vs Present Perfect

TenseUseSignalsExample
Present SimpleFacts, routines, permanent situationsevery day, usually, always, never"I specialize in endocrinology."
Present PerfectExperience, duration from past to nowfor, since, ever, never, already, yet"I have visited ninety-five countries."
Common Mistake

"I work since 2001." "I have worked here since 2001."

Use Present Perfect (not Present Simple) with "since" and "for" when the action continues to the present.

Stage 1.5: Application

Practice

Fill in the blanks with the correct word from the lesson.

"I in endocrinology."
"I focus on diabetes and obesity."
"My includes research in the United States."
"I want to my English fluency."
"I can my time in California very well."

Stage 2: Put in Order

Order

Put the sentences in the correct order.

? "I have been practicing for over twenty-five years."
? "I am an endocrinologist from Belem, Brazil."
? "I am here to reactivate my English fluency for international conferences."
? "Good morning, everyone. My name is Dr. Rubens Tofolo."
? "I specialize in diabetes and obesity management."
? "My background includes research in the United States and France."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen, record, and compare your pronunciation.

I specialize in endocrinology and obesity management.
I have been practicing for over twenty-five years.
I want to reactivate my English fluency.
It is nice to meet you. I am Dr. Tofolo from Brazil.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. You meet a colleague at a medical conference. How do you introduce yourself?
A "I am doctor. I do medicine things."
B "I am Dr. Tofolo. I specialize in endocrinology, and I am based in Belem, Brazil."
C "My name Rubens. I work hospital."
2. Someone asks: "How long have you been a doctor?" Which is the best answer?
A "I am doctor since twenty-five years."
B "I have been practicing medicine for over twenty-five years."
C "Twenty-five years I work as doctor."
3. A researcher asks about your international experience. What do you say?
A "I go many country."
B "I visited ninety-five countries yesterday."
C "I have visited ninety-five countries. My background includes research in the United States and France."
4. At a coffee break, someone asks why you are at this conference. What is the best response?
A "I want to reactivate my English fluency for international events. I used to present research in California."
B "My English is bad. I need to fix it."
C "I do not know why I am here."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection
Imagine you are at an international endocrinology conference. You meet a researcher from Japan during the coffee break. Record yourself introducing yourself professionally. Include: your name, your specialty, where you are based, how long you have been practicing, and one interesting fact about your background. Try to use at least 4 vocabulary words from this lesson.

Survival Card -- Lesson 1

1 Could you repeat that, please?
2 I specialize in endocrinology and obesity.
3 I have been practicing medicine for over twenty-five years.
4 Could you speak a little slower, please?
5 It is nice to meet you. I am Dr. Tofolo from Brazil.
Aula 02 -- Pre-class

Daily Routines & Clinic Life

Present Simple for routines and adverbs of frequency
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Learn the key words for this lesson. Click Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Routine -- a regular series of actions you do every day
"My morning routine starts at six o'clock."
Appointment -- a scheduled meeting with a patient or colleague
"I have six appointments every morning."
Commute -- the regular trip between home and work
"My commute to the clinic takes about thirty minutes."
Diagnosis -- the identification of a patient's illness or condition
"The diagnosis usually takes one consultation."
Schedule -- a plan of activities organized by time
"My schedule is always full on Mondays."
Prescribe -- to officially recommend a medicine or treatment
"I usually prescribe metformin for type two diabetes."
Colleague -- a person you work with professionally
"I often discuss cases with my colleagues."
Follow-up -- a return visit to check a patient's progress
"I always schedule a follow-up after the first consultation."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word to its correct definition.

Routine
Appointment
Commute
Diagnosis
Schedule
Prescribe
Colleague
Follow-up

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text below and answer the questions.

Dr. Tofolo always wakes up at five-thirty in the morning. He usually has coffee and reads medical journals before leaving home. His commute to the clinic takes about thirty minutes. He generally sees six to eight patients every morning. He often prescribes lifestyle changes alongside medication. After lunch, he sometimes meets with colleagues to discuss complex cases. He rarely finishes before seven in the evening. He never skips his morning exercise, even on the busiest days.
1. How often does Dr. Tofolo wake up at five-thirty?
A Sometimes
B Always -- it is part of his daily routine
C Only on weekdays
2. Which adverb of frequency tells us something happens NOT very often?
A Usually
B Rarely
C Often
3. "He never skips his morning exercise." What does this mean?
A He skips exercise sometimes
B He does not like exercise
C He exercises every single day, zero exceptions

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Present Simple for Routines + Adverbs of Frequency

StructureUseExample
Subject + verb(s)Facts, routines, habits"I wake up at five-thirty."
He/She + verb+sThird person singular adds -s"He prescribes medication daily."
Negative: do/does notNegation resets verb to base form"He does not skip exercise."
Question: Do/Does + subj + verb?Yes/no questions"Do you see patients every day?"
Adverbs of Frequency (position: before main verb, after "be")
always 100% usually 80% often 60% sometimes 40% rarely 10% never 0%
Common Mistake

"I go always to the clinic at seven." "I always go to the clinic at seven."

Adverbs of frequency go BEFORE the main verb (but AFTER the verb "be").

Stage 1.5: Application

Practice

Fill in the blanks with the correct word from the lesson.

"My morning starts at five-thirty."
"I have six every morning."
"My to the clinic takes thirty minutes."
"I usually metformin for type two diabetes."
"I always schedule a after the first consultation."

Stage 2: Vocabulary Practice

Practice

Complete the sentences using the vocabulary from this lesson.

"The confirmed type two diabetes."
"My is always full on Mondays."
"I discuss cases with my colleagues."
"He skips his morning exercise."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen, record, and compare your pronunciation.

I usually wake up at five-thirty and go to the clinic.
I always schedule a follow-up after the first consultation.
I often discuss complex cases with my colleagues.
I rarely finish before seven in the evening.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. A colleague asks: "What time do you usually start work?" What is the best answer?
A "I am starting usually at seven."
B "I usually start at seven in the morning. My first appointment is at seven-thirty."
C "Seven. Start. Morning."
2. A visiting doctor asks about your daily workload. What do you say?
A "I am seeing many patient every day."
B "Patients. Many. Busy."
C "I generally see six to eight patients every morning and schedule follow-ups in the afternoon."
3. Someone asks: "Do you exercise?" Which answer uses the correct adverb position?
A "Yes, I never skip my morning exercise. I always run before work."
B "Yes, I skip never my exercise morning."
C "Yes, always I am running before to work."
4. You are describing your clinic routine to a medical student. What do you say?
A "I am prescribing metformin always for the diabetes."
B "I usually prescribe metformin for type two diabetes, and I always schedule a follow-up."
C "Metformin I give. Follow-up I do."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection
Describe your typical workday from morning to evening. Include: what time you wake up, your commute, how many patients you see, what you do after lunch, and what time you finish. Use at least 4 adverbs of frequency (always, usually, often, sometimes, rarely, never) and 4 vocabulary words from this lesson.

Survival Card -- Lesson 2

1 I usually start my day at five-thirty in the morning.
2 I generally see six to eight patients every morning.
3 I always schedule a follow-up after the first consultation.
4 I often discuss cases with my colleagues after lunch.
5 I rarely finish before seven in the evening.
Aula 03 -- Pre-class

Talking About the Past

Past Simple for sharing memories and experiences
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Learn the key words for this lesson. Click Listen to hear the pronunciation.

Research -- a careful study to discover new facts or information
"I conducted research at a university in California."
Abroad -- in or to a foreign country
"I lived abroad for several years during my training."
Discover -- to find something new or learn about something for the first time
"I discovered my passion for endocrinology during my residency."
Publish -- to make a written work available for people to read
"I published my first paper in nineteen ninety-eight."
Experience -- something that happens to you that affects how you feel or think
"Living in California was an incredible experience."
Attend -- to go to and be present at an event or meeting
"I attended a major conference in San Francisco."
Opportunity -- a chance to do something valuable or beneficial
"The scholarship gave me the opportunity to study in France."
Achieve -- to succeed in reaching a goal through effort
"He achieved his doctorate in France at the age of thirty."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word to its correct definition.

Research
Abroad
Discover
Publish
Experience
Attend
Opportunity
Achieve

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the text below and answer the questions.

In nineteen ninety-five, Dr. Tofolo traveled to California for a research program. He lived there for two years and worked at a university hospital. He attended several international conferences and met researchers from around the world. He published three papers during that period. The experience changed his perspective on medicine. He discovered new approaches to metabolic syndrome. He achieved important results, and the opportunity opened doors for his career. He went back to Brazil in nineteen ninety-seven and brought all that knowledge to his clinic in Belem.
1. When did Dr. Tofolo travel to California?
A In nineteen ninety-five
B In two thousand and five
C Last year
2. Look at the bold verbs. Which form do they use?
A Present Simple (base form)
B Past Simple (finished actions in the past)
C Present Perfect (past connected to now)
3. "He went back to Brazil." Is "went" regular or irregular?
A Regular (go + ed = goed)
B Irregular (go → went)
C It is not Past Simple

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Grammar

Past Simple -- Regular and Irregular Verbs

TypeRuleBasePast
RegularAdd -edtravel, work, publishtraveled, worked, published
IrregularChanges formgo, meet, bringwent, met, brought
Negativedid not + base form--"He did not attend the meeting."
QuestionDid + subject + base?--"Did you live in California?"
Key Irregular Verbs for This Lesson
go → went meet → met bring → brought have → had get → got see → saw make → made take → took
Common Mistake

"I didn't went to the conference." "I didn't go to the conference."

After "did" or "did not," use the BASE form of the verb (never the past form).

Stage 1.5: Application

Practice

Fill in the blanks with the correct past form of the verb.

"I to California in nineteen ninety-five."
"I three papers during that period."
"I researchers from around the world."
"The experience my perspective on medicine."
"I back to Brazil in nineteen ninety-seven."

Stage 2: Vocabulary Practice

Practice

Complete the sentences using the vocabulary from this lesson.

"I my passion for endocrinology during my residency."
"I a major conference in San Francisco."
"The scholarship gave me the to study in France."
"He his doctorate in France at the age of thirty."

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen, record, and compare your pronunciation.

I traveled to California in nineteen ninety-five.
I met researchers from around the world.
I published three papers during that period.
The experience changed my perspective on medicine.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz
1. Someone asks: "Did you live in the United States?" What is the best answer?
A "Yes, I lived. California. Two years."
B "Yes, I lived in California for two years. I conducted research at a university hospital."
C "Yes, I live in California since nineteen ninety-five."
2. A colleague asks about your time in France. Which sentence is correct?
A "I did my PhD and I didn't went back for three years."
B "I achieved my doctorate in France. The opportunity changed my career."
C "I achieve doctorate in France. Opportunity change career."
3. You want to say you published papers. Which is correct?
A "I published three papers during my time in California."
B "I publicated three papers during my time in California."
C "I have publish three papers during my time in California."
4. Someone asks: "What did you discover in California?" How do you answer?
A "I discover new approach to metabolic syndrome."
B "I did discovered new approaches."
C "I discovered new approaches to metabolic syndrome that changed my practice."

Stage 5: Think About It

Reflection
Think about one important experience from your past that changed your career or your life. Record yourself telling the story using Past Simple. Include: when it happened, where you were, what you did, who you met, and how it changed you. Try to use at least 4 vocabulary words from this lesson and mix regular and irregular past verbs.

Survival Card -- Lesson 3

1 I traveled to California in nineteen ninety-five.
2 I lived abroad for two years and conducted research.
3 I published three papers during that period.
4 The experience changed my perspective on medicine.
5 I achieved my doctorate in France at the age of thirty.
Aula 04 -- Pre-class

Life Experiences

Present Perfect for sharing life experiences with ever, never, already, yet
0%
▼
Vocabulary

Stage 1.1: Key Vocabulary

Learn 8 words connected to life experiences and personal achievements.

Journey -- A long trip or the process of personal growth
"My journey to becoming a doctor started in medical school."
Explore -- To travel or search for new places or ideas
"I want to explore new treatment methods for diabetes."
Milestone -- An important event or achievement in life
"Getting my doctorate was a major milestone in my career."
Challenge -- Something difficult that tests your ability
"Learning seven languages was a real challenge."
Accomplish -- To succeed in doing something important
"I have accomplished many things in twenty-five years of medicine."
Broaden -- To make wider, to expand knowledge or perspective
"Traveling has broadened my perspective on medicine."
Overcome -- To succeed in dealing with a problem or difficulty
"I have overcome many obstacles in my career."
Remarkable -- Worthy of attention; unusual or impressive
"His career has been truly remarkable."
Practice

Stage 1.2: Match the Words

Match each word to its correct definition.

Journey
Explore
Milestone
Challenge
Accomplish
Broaden
Overcome
Remarkable
Grammar

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Read the text and answer the questions.

Dr. Tofolo has visited ninety-five countries in his lifetime. He has never missed a major medical conference, and he has already registered for three events next year. His colleagues say his career has been truly remarkable.

He has explored different cultures, has overcome language barriers, and has accomplished things that most people only dream of. When asked about his biggest milestone, he says: "Getting my doctorate in France. It has broadened my perspective on everything."

"Have you ever felt lost in a foreign country?" a colleague once asked. "Many times," Rubens replied, "but every challenge has made my journey more interesting."

1. How many countries has Dr. Tofolo visited?
A Fifty-five countries
B Ninety-five countries
C Twenty-five countries
2. What does Rubens consider his biggest milestone?
A Visiting ninety-five countries
B Learning seven languages
C Getting his doctorate in France
3. Has Rubens ever missed a major medical conference?
A No, he has never missed one
B Yes, he missed one in Tokyo
C The text does not say
Grammar

Grammar Tip: Present Perfect

Present Perfect -- have/has + past participle

Form Structure Example
Affirmative Subject + have/has + past participle I have visited ninety-five countries.
Negative Subject + have/has + not + past participle I have never missed a deadline.
Question Have/Has + subject + past participle? Have you ever lived abroad?

Key time markers:

ever = at any time in life (questions) never = at no time (negatives) already = before now (done) yet = until now (not done) just = very recently

Present Perfect vs Past Simple:

"I have visited France." = life experience, no specific time

"I visited France in 1990." = specific past time

Erro comum: "I have visited France in 1990." Present Perfect NUNCA usa tempo específico (in 1990, yesterday, last week). Com tempo específico, use Past Simple.

Practice

Stage 1.5: Complete the Sentences

Fill in the blanks with the correct form of the verb in Present Perfect.

"I ninety-five countries."
"Have you attended a medical conference abroad?"
"I have published several papers on metabolic syndrome."
"I have missed a conference deadline."
"I haven't visited Africa ."
Practice

Stage 2: Vocabulary in Action

Use the vocabulary from this lesson to complete the sentences.

"Getting my doctorate was a major in my career."
"Traveling has my perspective on medicine."
"Learning seven languages was a real ."
"His career has been truly ."
Speaking

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

I have visited ninety-five countries.
Have you ever attended a medical conference abroad?
I have already published several papers on metabolic syndrome.
Traveling has broadened my perspective on medicine.
Quiz

Stage 4: Choose the Best Response

Select the most natural answer for each situation.

1. A colleague asks: "Have you ever been to Japan?"
A "Yes, I have. I visited Tokyo for a conference in 2018."
B "Yes, I have been to Japan yesterday."
C "Yes, I ever been to Japan."
2. Someone asks about your research publications.
A "I have already publish three papers."
B "I have already published three papers on metabolic syndrome."
C "I already have published three papers."
3. A friend asks if you have finished preparing for the conference.
A "No, I didn't finished yet."
B "No, I haven't finish yet."
C "No, I haven't finished yet."
4. You want to say that you have never lost a patient to a preventable cause.
A "I never have lost a patient."
B "I have never lost a patient to a preventable cause."
C "I have ever not lost a patient."
Reflection

Stage 5: Think About It

Think about your life experiences. Answer this question using Present Perfect: "What are three remarkable things you have accomplished in your career?" Record your answer.
Example: "I have accomplished many things. First, I have visited ninety-five countries. Second, I have already published several papers on metabolic syndrome. Third, I have overcome the challenge of learning seven languages."

Survival Card -- Lesson 4

1 I have visited ninety-five countries.
2 Have you ever attended a medical conference abroad?
3 I have already published several papers on metabolic syndrome.
4 I have never missed a conference deadline.
5 Traveling has broadened my perspective on medicine.
Aula 5 -- Pre-class

Describing People & Places

Descreva sua cidade e seu mundo: crowded, humid, lively, charming, historic, affordable, welcoming, breathtaking. Estruturas: comparativos ("hotter than", "more lively than") e superlativos ("the biggest", "the most welcoming", "the best"). Missão: descrever Belém para uma estrangeira.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Crowded -- full of people, with little space
"The market is crowded on Saturdays." (Cheio / lotado)
Humid -- hot air, full of water
"Belém is hot and humid all year." (Úmido)
Lively -- full of life, energy, and movement
"The Ver-o-Peso market is very lively." (Animado / cheio de vida)
Charming -- beautiful in a delicate, attractive way
"The old town is small but charming." (Encantador)
Historic -- old and important in history
"Belém has a historic city center." (Histórico)
Affordable -- not expensive
"Life in Belém is more affordable than in Lisbon." (Acessível / em conta)
Welcoming -- friendly to visitors
"People in the North are very welcoming." (Acolhedor)
Breathtaking -- so beautiful it stops your breath
"The sunset over the river is breathtaking." (De tirar o fôlego)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Crowded
Humid
Lively
Charming
Historic
Affordable
Welcoming
Breathtaking

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

At the coffee break, Dr. Sofia asks about Rubens' city. "Belém is hotter than Lisbon, and much more humid," he says. "The Ver-o-Peso is the biggest open-air market in Latin America — crowded but charming. And our açaí is better than any açaí in the South. People say the sunset over the river is the most beautiful in Brazil. For me, it is simply breathtaking."

1. Como Rubens compara o clima? / How does Rubens compare the weather?
A Belém is more hot than Lisbon.
B Belém is hotter than Lisbon.
C Belém is the hotter of Lisbon.
2. O que é o Ver-o-Peso? / What is the Ver-o-Peso?
A The biggest open-air market in Latin America.
B The most expensive restaurant in Belém.
C A historic tower in Lisbon.
3. Como ele descreve o pôr do sol? / How does he describe the sunset?
A Crowded.
B Affordable.
C Breathtaking.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Comparatives & Superlatives

GRAMMAR

Comparar duas coisas e dizer o nº 1 do grupo. / Compare two things and say the number one.

AdjetivoComparativo / ComparativeSuperlativo / Superlative
curto: hot, bighotter / bigger than (mais ... que)the hottest / biggest (o mais ...)
longo: lively, affordablemore lively thanthe most lively
irregular: good, badbetter / worse than (melhor/pior que)the best / the worst (o melhor/pior)

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Belém is than Lisbon."
"The market is lively than the mall."
"The Amazon is the forest in the world."
"Our açaí is than the açaí in São Paulo."
"People from the North are the most in Brazil."
"The sunset over the river is ."

Stage 2: Put the Steps in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the description in the correct order.

?He compares the prices: Belém is more affordable.
?Sofia asks Rubens about his city.
?Sofia decides she wants to visit Belém.
?He describes the weather: hotter and more humid than Lisbon.
?He talks about the best things: the market and the sunset.

Stage 3: Pronunciation Practice

Speaking

Pratique as frases que você vai usar para descrever Belém.

Belém is hotter than Lisbon.
It is the biggest market in Latin America.
People from the North are the most welcoming in Brazil.
The sunset over the river is breathtaking.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

Uma colega pergunta se Belém é como Lisboa. / A colleague asks if Belém is like Lisbon.
A "Not really. Belém is hotter and more humid than Lisbon."
B "Belém is more hot that Lisbon."
C "Lisbon and Belém is equal."
Você quer dizer que o Ver-o-Peso é o nº 1 em tamanho. / You want to say Ver-o-Peso is number one in size.
A "It is the most big market."
B "It is the biggest open-air market in Latin America."
C "It is more bigger than all."
Ela pergunta sobre o povo do Norte. / She asks about the people of the North.
A "They are the more welcoming."
B "They are the most welcoming people in Brazil."
C "They are welcomest."
Você quer convencê-la a visitar. / You want to convince her to visit.
A "Come! The sunset over the river is breathtaking."
B "Come, the city is very crowded."
C "Come, it rains every afternoon."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

No right or wrong here. Think in English. Tap the mic and speak freely.

Describe Belém to a foreigner: the weather, the Ver-o-Peso market, the food, the people, and the sunset. Compare it with another city you know. Use: hotter than, more ... than, the biggest, the most welcoming, the best.

Survival Card -- Lesson 5

1Belém is hotter than Lisbon.Belém é mais quente que Lisboa.
2The city is lively and welcoming.A cidade é animada e acolhedora.
3It is the biggest market in Latin America.É o maior mercado da América Latina.
4Life here is more affordable.A vida aqui é mais em conta.
5The sunset over the river is breathtaking.O pôr do sol sobre o rio é de tirar o fôlego.
Aula 06 -- Pre-class

Making Plans -- Your Congress Trip

Talk about future plans and decisions with going to (a plan you already decided) and will (a decision, an offer, a prediction). New vocabulary for travel and events: itinerary, departure, booking, deadline, arrange, venue, keynote, registration, luggage, confirm, upcoming, postpone.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Itinerary -- a detailed plan of a journey, day by day
"My itinerary shows three cities in five days." (Itinerário)
Departure -- the moment you leave, especially a flight
"My departure is at six in the morning." (Partida)
Booking -- a reservation you make in advance
"I made a booking at a hotel near the venue." (Reserva)
Deadline -- the latest time to finish something
"The registration deadline is next Friday." (Prazo final)
Arrange -- to plan and organize something
"I will arrange the transport from the airport." (Organizar)
Venue -- the place where an event happens
"The congress venue is a large convention center." (Local do evento)
Keynote -- the most important talk at a conference
"A famous researcher will give the keynote." (Palestra principal)
Registration -- the act of signing up for an event officially
"Registration opens two months before the event." (Inscrição)
Luggage -- the bags you take when you travel
"I am going to take light luggage this time." (Bagagem)
Confirm -- to say that something is definite and final
"I will confirm my booking tomorrow." (Confirmar)
Upcoming -- happening soon in the near future
"The upcoming congress is in October." (Próximo)
Postpone -- to move an event to a later time
"They had to postpone the workshop until May." (Adiar)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Itinerary
Departure
Booking
Deadline
Arrange
Venue
Keynote
Registration
Luggage
Confirm
Upcoming
Postpone

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Rubens is planning his trip to the upcoming endocrinology congress. "I am going to attend the congress in Lisbon next month," he says. The decision is already made, so he uses going to. He has a clear plan: "First, I am going to finish my registration before the deadline. I already have a booking near the venue." Then his phone rings. The coordinator asks about transport from the airport. Rubens thinks for a second and decides on the spot: "Do not worry, I will arrange a taxi myself." This is a decision he makes at the moment of speaking, so he uses will. Before the call ends, he makes a promise: "I will confirm everything by Friday." He also gives an opinion about the trip: "I think the keynote will be excellent."

1. Why does Rubens say "I am going to attend the congress"?
A Because it is a plan he already decided.
B Because he is deciding it right now.
C Because it already happened in the past.
2. Why does he say "I will arrange a taxi" with will?
A Because he decides it at the moment of speaking.
B Because it was a plan from last week.
C Because the taxi is already booked.
3. When Rubens says "the keynote will be excellent", he is giving a...
A prediction or opinion about the future.
B fixed plan in his diary.
C description of the past.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Going to vs Will

GRAMMAR

Usado para falar do futuro. / Used to talk about the future.

Form / FormaUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
am/is/are going to + verbPlano já decidido antes de falar / a plan already decidedI am going to attend the congress.
going to + verbPrevisão com evidência / prediction with evidenceLook at the clouds -- it is going to rain.
will + verbDecisão na hora de falar / decision at the momentThe phone is ringing. I will answer it.
will + verbOferta ou promessa / offer or promiseI will confirm the booking tomorrow.
I think / I am sure + willOpinião ou previsão / opinion or predictionI think the keynote will be great.

Key point / Ponto-chave: depois de will use o verbo sem to (will confirm, não will to confirm). Use going to para um plano que você já tinha; use will para uma decisão, oferta ou opinião no momento.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"My shows three cities in five days."
"The registration is next Friday."
"I made a at a hotel near the venue."
"A famous researcher will give the ."
"I will my booking tomorrow."
"I am going to take light this time."

Stage 2: Order the Itinerary

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of the plan in the correct order.

?After that, I am going to buy my flight and check my departure time.
?First, I am going to complete my registration before the deadline.
?Finally, on the first morning, I am going to attend the keynote.
?Then I will confirm my hotel booking near the venue.
?Next, I will prepare my luggage the night before.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

I am going to attend the upcoming congress in Lisbon.
Eu vou participar do próximo congresso em Lisboa.
Do not worry, I will arrange the transport from the airport.
Não se preocupe, eu vou organizar o transporte do aeroporto.
I will confirm my booking before the deadline.
Eu vou confirmar minha reserva antes do prazo final.
My departure is early, so I will take light luggage.
Minha partida é cedo, então vou levar pouca bagagem.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You already decided last week to go to the congress. How do you say it?
A "I am going to go to the congress."
B "I will go to the congress." (just now decided)
C "I go to the congress yesterday."
The coordinator asks who will book the taxi, and you decide right now. What do you say?
A "I will book it."
B "I am going to booking it."
C "I will to book it."
Which sentence is correct after will?
A "I will confirm the venue tomorrow."
B "I will confirming the venue tomorrow."
C "I will to confirm the venue tomorrow."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Plan your next trip to a congress or a holiday in one minute. Use going to for the plans you already decided (I am going to book..., I am going to attend...) and will for decisions, offers, and predictions (I will confirm..., I think it will be...). Mention your registration, your booking, your departure, and one thing you will do when you arrive.

Survival Card -- Lesson 6

1I am going to attend the upcoming congress next month.Eu vou participar do próximo congresso no mês que vem.
2I will confirm my booking before the deadline.Eu vou confirmar minha reserva antes do prazo final.
3Do not worry, I will arrange the transport.Não se preocupe, eu vou organizar o transporte.
4My departure is early, so I will take light luggage.Minha partida é cedo, então vou levar pouca bagagem.
5I think the keynote will be excellent.Eu acho que a palestra principal vai ser excelente.
Aula 07 -- Pre-class

At the Restaurant -- Ordering Politely

Order food politely with would like (a polite request), could / can I have (a polite question) and I will have (a decision when ordering). New restaurant vocabulary: appetizer, main course, side dish, dessert, beverage, recommend, server, bill, tip, spicy, allergy, refill.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Appetizer -- a small dish served before the main course
"I would like an appetizer to start."
Main course -- the largest and most important dish of a meal
"For my main course, I will have the lamb."
Side dish -- a small dish served together with the main course
"The main course comes with a side dish of rice."
Dessert -- sweet food eaten at the end of a meal
"For dessert, the chocolate cake is excellent."
Beverage -- any kind of drink
"Every main course includes one free beverage."
Recommend -- to suggest something good to someone
"What do you recommend for tonight?"
Server -- the person who takes your order in a restaurant
"I will ask the server for the menu."
Bill -- the paper that shows how much you must pay
"Could I have the bill, please?"
Tip -- extra money you leave for good service
"I always leave a tip for good service."
Spicy -- having a strong, hot flavor
"I prefer a dish that is not too spicy."
Allergy -- a bad reaction of the body to a certain food
"I have an allergy to nuts."
Refill -- a second serving of the same drink, often free
"The restaurant offers a free refill of water."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Appetizer
Main course
Side dish
Dessert
Beverage
Recommend
Server
Bill
Tip
Spicy
Allergy
Refill

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

After a long day at the congress, Rubens sits down at a restaurant in Lisbon. The server comes to his table, and he is polite. First he asks, "Could I have the menu, please?" He reads it and decides. "I would like the grilled shrimp to start," he says, "and for the main course, what do you recommend?" The server suggests the roasted lamb. "Perfect, I will have the lamb," Rubens answers. He also mentions an important detail: "I have an allergy to nuts, so please be careful." At the end of the meal, he does not say "give me the bill." Instead, he asks politely: "Could I have the bill, please?" A polite request always sounds better, and the service is friendlier because of it.

1. How does Rubens ask for the menu?
A "Could I have the menu, please?"
B "Give me the menu now."
C "Menu, fast."
2. Why does Rubens say "I would like" instead of "I want"?
A Because it is more polite.
B Because it is in the past.
C Because it is a question.
3. What does Rubens say instead of "give me the bill"?
A "Could I have the bill, please?"
B "Bring the bill, you must."
C "I am going to take the bill."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Ordering Politely

GRAMMAR

Usado para pedir comida com educação. / Used to order food politely.

Form / FormaUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
I would like + nounPedido educado / a polite requestI would like an appetizer.
I would like + to + verbPedido educado (ação) / polite request (action)I would like to order now.
Could / Can I have + noun?Pergunta educada / a polite questionCould I have the menu?
I will have + nounDecisão ao pedir / a decision when orderingI will have the lamb.
What do you recommend?Pedir sugestão / asking for adviceWhat do you recommend tonight?

Key point / Ponto-chave: would like é mais educado que want. Depois de could / can I have, use um substantivo (could I have the menu, não could I have to the menu). Termine pedidos com please.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"I an appetizer to start, please."
" I have the menu, please?"
"What do you for the main course?"
"I have the chocolate cake for dessert."
"Could I have a of water, please?"
"Please tell the server about your ."

Stage 2: Order the Steps

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of ordering a meal in the correct order.

?Finally, when I finish my dessert, I ask for the bill and leave a tip.
?First, I read the menu and choose my dishes.
?Next, I mention my allergy so the kitchen is careful.
?Then I ask the server what they recommend.
?After that, I order an appetizer and a main course.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

I would like an appetizer and a main course, please.
Eu gostaria de uma entrada e um prato principal, por favor.
Could I have a beverage with no ice?
Posso pedir uma bebida sem gelo?
I have an allergy to nuts, so please be careful.
Eu tenho alergia a castanhas, então por favor tenha cuidado.
Could I have the bill, please? The dinner was excellent.
Posso pedir a conta, por favor? O jantar estava excelente.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want to order your main course politely. What do you say?
A "I would like the roasted lamb, please."
B "Give me the lamb."
C "I will to have the lamb."
You want the server to suggest something. What do you ask?
A "What do you recommend?"
B "What you recommend?"
C "Recommend me, what?"
You finished eating and want to pay. What do you say?
A "Could I have the bill, please?"
B "I want bill now."
C "Where is bill?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are at a restaurant during your next trip. Order a full meal in one minute. Use would like and could I have for polite requests (I would like an appetizer..., could I have a beverage...), ask the server for advice (what do you recommend?), and mention any allergy. At the end, ask for the bill politely and say something about the tip.

Survival Card -- Lesson 7

1I would like to see the menu, please.Eu gostaria de ver o cardápio, por favor.
2Could I have a beverage and a side dish?Posso pedir uma bebida e um acompanhamento?
3What do you recommend tonight?O que você recomenda hoje à noite?
4I have an allergy, so please no nuts.Eu tenho alergia, então por favor sem castanhas.
5Could I have the bill, please?Posso pedir a conta, por favor?
Aula 08 -- Pre-class

Block 1 Review -- Reflect on Your Progress

Look back at Block 1 and describe your learning with the present perfect (I have improved), for (a period) and since (a starting point). New reflection vocabulary: progress, improve, confident, goal, reflect, review, struggle, mistake, feedback, strength, weakness, steady.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Progress -- forward movement toward something better
"My progress is slow but steady."
Improve -- to become better or make something better
"I have improved a lot since Lesson 1."
Confident -- sure of yourself and your ability
"I feel more confident when I speak."
Goal -- something you want to achieve in the future
"My goal is to speak at a congress."
Reflect -- to think carefully about your experience
"I reflect on each lesson at the end of the week."
Review -- to look at something again to remember it
"I review my notes every morning."
Struggle -- to find something difficult and try hard
"I still struggle with the past tense."
Mistake -- something you do wrong by accident
"I make a few mistakes, but I learn from them."
Feedback -- helpful comments about your performance
"I ask my teacher for feedback after each class."
Strength -- something you do very well
"My strength is that I am not afraid to speak."
Weakness -- something you need to work on and improve
"My weakness is the past tense."
Steady -- slow, regular and continuous, without stopping
"My progress is slow and steady."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Progress
Improve
Confident
Goal
Reflect
Review
Struggle
Mistake
Feedback
Strength
Weakness
Steady

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

At the end of Block 1, Rubens sits down for his first review. He looks back and reflects on his progress. "I have studied English for six months," he thinks, "and I have improved a lot since Lesson 1." His confidence has grown every week, and his biggest strength is that he is not afraid to speak. Of course, he still has a weakness: he has struggled with the past tense, and he sometimes makes a small mistake. His teacher gives honest feedback, so he reviews his notes a little every day. "Last week I finished Lesson 7," he says, "and now I have a clear goal: to speak with confidence at a medical congress." His progress is slow and steady, but it is real.

1. How long has Rubens studied English?
A He has studied for six months.
B He studied since six months.
C He has study six months ago.
2. What is his weakness?
A He has struggled with the past tense.
B He is afraid to speak.
C He never reviews his notes.
3. Why does he say "Last week I finished Lesson 7" in the simple past?
A Because the time is finished (last week).
B Because it is happening now.
C Because there is no exact time.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Perfect for Progress

GRAMMAR

Usado para falar do seu progresso ate agora. / Used to talk about your progress up to now.

Form / FormaUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
have / has + past participleResultado ate agora / a result up to nowI have improved a lot.
for + a periodDuracao / how longI have studied for six months.
since + a point in timePonto de inicio / a starting pointI have studied since March.
How long have you + participle?Pergunta / a questionHow long have you studied?
simple past + finished timeMomento terminado / a finished momentLast month I started Block 1.

Key point / Ponto-chave: depois de have / has, use o particicio passado (improved, grown, learned). Use for com um periodo (for six months) e since com um ponto de inicio (since March). Use o passado simples quando o tempo terminou (last month).

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"I have a lot this month."
"My has been slow and steady."
"I have studied English six months."
"I ask my teacher for after each class."
"My biggest is the past tense."
"My is to speak at a congress."

Stage 2: Order the Steps

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of reviewing your progress in the correct order.

?Finally, I review a little every day to keep my progress steady.
?First, I look back at the lessons I have finished.
?Next, I ask my teacher for feedback and practice the difficult parts.
?Then I write down my strengths and my weaknesses.
?After that, I choose one small goal for the next class.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

I have improved a lot since I started Block 1.
Eu melhorei muito desde que comecei o Bloco 1.
My progress is slow and steady, but it is real.
Meu progresso e lento e constante, mas e real.
Could you give me some feedback on my speaking?
Voce poderia me dar um retorno sobre a minha fala?
My goal is to speak with more confidence.
Minha meta e falar com mais confianca.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want to say you got better up to now. What do you say?
A "I have improved a lot."
B "I have improve a lot."
C "I improving a lot."
You want to give the duration of your study. What do you say?
A "I have studied for six months."
B "I have studied since six months."
C "I study for six months ago."
You want to ask your teacher for helpful comments. What do you say?
A "Could you give me some feedback, please?"
B "Give me feedback now."
C "Where is my feedback?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Reflect on your Block 1 in one minute. Use the present perfect to say what you have done and improved (I have studied for..., I have improved...), name one strength and one weakness, and finish with your next goal for Block 2.

Survival Card -- Lesson 8

1I have improved a lot since Lesson 1.Eu melhorei muito desde a Aula 1.
2I have studied English for six months.Eu estudo ingles ha seis meses.
3My strength is speaking, and my weakness is the past tense.Meu ponto forte e falar, e meu ponto fraco e o passado.
4Could you give me some feedback, please?Voce poderia me dar um retorno, por favor?
5My goal is to speak with more confidence.Minha meta e falar com mais confianca.
Aula 09 -- Pre-class

Healthy Habits -- Giving Advice

Give healthy-living advice with should, shouldn't, must and mustn't. New vocabulary: habit, balanced diet, workout, stress, symptom, exhausted, recover, wellbeing, prevent, routine.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Habit -- something you do regularly, almost automatically
"Sleeping well is a good habit." (Hábito)
Balanced diet -- eating different healthy foods in the right amount
"A balanced diet has vegetables and fruit." (Dieta equilibrada)
Workout -- a session of physical exercise
"A short workout reduces stress." (Treino)
Stress -- mental or physical tension and pressure
"Too much stress is bad for your health." (Estresse)
Symptom -- a sign that shows you may have a health problem
"Tiredness can be a symptom of stress." (Sintoma)
Exhausted -- extremely tired, with no energy
"I feel exhausted after a long shift." (Exausto)
Recover -- to get your health or energy back
"Good sleep helps you recover." (Recuperar)
Wellbeing -- the state of being healthy, calm and happy
"Exercise improves your wellbeing." (Bem-estar)
Prevent -- to stop a problem before it happens
"Healthy habits prevent many problems." (Prevenir)
Routine -- a fixed order of things you do every day
"A morning routine keeps me calm." (Rotina)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Habit
Balanced diet
Workout
Stress
Symptom
Exhausted
Recover
Wellbeing
Prevent
Routine

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Doctor Rubens gives his patients simple advice. "You should sleep at least seven hours, because good sleep helps your body recover. You should also eat a balanced diet and do a short workout. You shouldn't skip your meals, and you mustn't ignore a symptom like feeling exhausted all the time. To reduce stress, you must find time to relax. These small habits prevent big problems and improve your wellbeing."

1. Why should you sleep at least seven hours?
A Because good sleep helps your body recover.
B Because you must skip meals.
C Because stress is good.
2. What mustn't you ignore?
A A balanced diet.
B A symptom like feeling exhausted all the time.
C A short workout.
3. What do these small habits do?
A They prevent big problems and improve your wellbeing.
B They cause more stress.
C They make you exhausted.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- should, shouldn't, must, mustn't

GRAMMAR

Usamos esses modais para dar conselhos e falar de obrigação. / We use these modals to give advice and talk about obligation.

ModalUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
shouldConselho (advice / a good idea)You should sleep well
shouldn'tConselho negativo (not a good idea)You shouldn't skip meals
mustObrigação forte / necessidadeYou must drink water
mustn'tProibição (it is prohibited)You mustn't ignore a symptom

Key point / Ponto-chave: depois desses modais use sempre o verbo no infinitivo sem "to": should sleep, must drink (nunca "should to sleep").

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"You sleep at least seven hours."
"You skip your meals."
"You find time to relax."
"You ignore your symptoms."
"A short reduces stress."
"Healthy habits many problems."

Stage 2: Put the Routine in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of the morning routine in the correct order.

?After that, I do a short workout to reduce my stress.
?First, I go to bed early so I can sleep well.
?Finally, I take ten minutes to relax and plan a calm day.
?Then I drink a large glass of water when I wake up.
?Next, I eat a balanced breakfast with fruit.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

You should sleep at least seven hours.
Você deveria dormir pelo menos sete horas.
You shouldn't skip your meals.
Você não deveria pular as refeições.
You must find time to relax.
Você precisa arrumar tempo para relaxar.
A short workout can improve your wellbeing.
Um treino curto pode melhorar o seu bem-estar.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best advice for each situation.

A patient feels exhausted and stressed every day. What is the best advice?
A "You must work more hours."
B "You should sleep more and find time to relax."
C "You shouldn't drink water."
You want to tell a patient that ignoring symptoms is dangerous. Which is best?
A "You mustn't ignore your symptoms."
B "You should ignore your symptoms."
C "You mustn't see a doctor."
A friend eats only fast food. What healthy advice can you give?
A "You should skip your meals."
B "You mustn't eat vegetables."
C "You should eat a more balanced diet."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine a patient asks you for advice on healthy habits. Give three pieces of advice out loud. Use should, shouldn't and must at least once, and two new words (balanced diet, workout, stress, wellbeing).

Survival Card -- Lesson 9

1You should sleep at least seven hours.Você deveria dormir pelo menos sete horas.
2You shouldn't skip your meals.Você não deveria pular as refeições.
3You must find time to relax.Você precisa arrumar tempo para relaxar.
4You mustn't ignore your symptoms.Você não deve ignorar os seus sintomas.
5Healthy habits prevent many problems.Hábitos saudáveis previnem muitos problemas.
Aula 10 -- Pre-class

Giving Directions

Give and follow directions with imperatives and prepositions of place. New vocabulary: go straight, turn left, turn right, corner, next to, between, opposite, floor, elevator, hallway.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Go straight -- to keep moving forward without turning
"Go straight down the corridor." (Siga em frente)
Turn left -- to change direction to your left side
"Turn left at the corner." (Vire à esquerda)
Turn right -- to change direction to your right side
"Turn right after the pharmacy." (Vire à direita)
Corner -- the point where two walls or streets meet
"The elevator is around the corner." (Esquina / canto)
Next to -- directly beside something
"The pharmacy is next to the elevator." (Ao lado de)
Between -- in the middle of two things
"Reception is between the two doors." (Entre)
Opposite -- directly across from something
"The department is opposite the laboratory." (Em frente a)
Floor -- a level of a building
"My office is on the second floor." (Andar)
Elevator -- a machine that carries you between floors
"Take the elevator to the third floor." (Elevador)
Hallway -- a long passage inside a building
"Walk down the hallway to the end." (Corredor)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Go straight
Turn left
Turn right
Corner
Next to
Between
Opposite
Floor
Elevator
Hallway

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Doctor Rubens helps a lost patient at the hospital. "Go straight down this hallway and turn left at the corner. The elevator is on your right, next to the pharmacy. Take the elevator to the third floor. The cardiology department is at the end of the hall, opposite the laboratory. The reception desk is between the two doors. Please do not use the stairs on the left, because they are closed today."

1. Where do you turn left?
A At the corner of the hallway.
B At the reception desk.
C On the third floor.
2. What is next to the elevator?
A The laboratory.
B The pharmacy.
C The stairs.
3. Why mustn't you use the stairs on the left?
A Because they are closed today.
B Because they are on the third floor.
C Because they are next to the pharmacy.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- imperatives & prepositions of place

GRAMMAR

Usamos o imperativo para dar instruções e preposições de lugar para dizer onde algo está. / We use the imperative to give instructions and prepositions of place to say where something is.

FormUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
ImperativeInstrução (base verb, no subject)Go straight. Turn left.
Negative imperativeInstrução negativaDo not use the stairs
next to / betweenPosição (ao lado / entre)It is next to the elevator
opposite / behindPosição (em frente / atrás)It is opposite the lab

Key point / Ponto-chave: o imperativo começa com o verbo base, sem "to" e sem sujeito: Go straight, Turn left (nunca "To turn left" nem "You turn left").

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

" straight down the corridor."
" left at the corner."
"The pharmacy is to the elevator."
"The department is the laboratory."
"Take the elevator to the third ."
"Do not the stairs on the left."

Stage 2: Put the Directions in Order

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of the route in the correct order.

?After that, take the elevator to the second floor.
?First, go through the main door and turn right.
?Finally, my office is the second door on your right, opposite the laboratory.
?Then walk straight ahead to the end of the corridor.
?Next, turn left when you leave the elevator.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

Go straight down the corridor.
Siga em frente pelo corredor.
Turn left at the corner.
Vire à esquerda na esquina.
Take the elevator to the third floor.
Pegue o elevador até o terceiro andar.
The pharmacy is next to the elevator.
A farmácia fica ao lado do elevador.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best direction for each situation.

A patient at the entrance wants the pharmacy, which is next to the elevator. What do you say?
A "The pharmacy is on the third floor."
B "Go straight, and the pharmacy is next to the elevator."
C "Turn left and use the stairs."
You want to warn a visitor that the stairs on the left are closed. Which is best?
A "Do not use the stairs on the left."
B "Use the stairs on the left."
C "The stairs are next to the third floor."
A patient asks where the cardiology department is. It is across from the laboratory. What do you say?
A "It is next the laboratory."
B "It is behind the entrance."
C "It is opposite the laboratory."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine a patient calls and asks how to get to your office. Give clear directions out loud, step by step. Use go, turn and take, and at least two prepositions of place (next to, between, opposite, behind).

Survival Card -- Lesson 10

1Go straight down the corridor.Siga em frente pelo corredor.
2Turn left at the corner.Vire à esquerda na esquina.
3Take the elevator to the third floor.Pegue o elevador até o terceiro andar.
4The pharmacy is next to the elevator.A farmácia fica ao lado do elevador.
5The department is opposite the laboratory.O departamento fica em frente ao laboratório.
Aula 11 -- Pre-class

Shopping & Services

Buy medical supplies with how much, how many and countable/uncountable nouns. New vocabulary: customer, cashier, receipt, discount, refund, supplies, quantity, stock, brand, cash.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Customer -- a person who buys goods or services
"The customer wants to buy gloves." (Cliente)
Cashier -- the person who takes your money at a store
"Pay the cashier at the front desk." (Caixa)
Receipt -- a paper that shows what you bought and paid
"Keep the receipt for your records." (Recibo)
Discount -- a reduction in the price of something
"They give a discount on large orders." (Desconto)
Refund -- money returned when you give a product back
"I asked for a refund on the wrong item." (Reembolso)
Supplies -- the materials and items you need for work
"The clinic needs more medical supplies." (Suprimentos)
Quantity -- how much or how many of something there is
"What quantity of masks do you need?" (Quantidade)
Stock -- the goods a store has available to sell
"The gloves are in stock this week." (Estoque)
Brand -- the name of a company that makes a product
"I prefer this brand of gloves." (Marca)
Cash -- money in coins and notes (not a card)
"You can pay in cash or by card." (Dinheiro)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Customer
Cashier
Receipt
Discount
Refund
Supplies
Quantity
Stock
Brand
Cash

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Doctor Rubens orders supplies for his clinic. He asks the cashier: "How many boxes of gloves do you have in stock? And how much alcohol is left?" The cashier says the gloves are on sale, so he gets a discount. Rubens needs a few masks and a little cotton too. He prefers one brand because it is affordable. At the end, he pays in cash and asks for the receipt.

1. Which question does Rubens use for the gloves?
A How many boxes of gloves do you have?
B How much boxes of gloves do you have?
C How long boxes of gloves do you have?
2. Why does the cashier give a discount?
A Because Rubens pays by card.
B Because the gloves are on sale.
C Because the cotton is out of stock.
3. How does Rubens pay?
A By card, and he forgets the receipt.
B In cash, and he asks for the receipt.
C With a refund from another store.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- how much / how many & countable / uncountable

GRAMMAR

Usamos how many com substantivos contáveis e how much com incontáveis. / We use how many with countable nouns and how much with uncountable nouns.

Noun typeUse / UsoExample / Exemplo
Countablehow many / a few (gloves, masks)How many gloves? A few masks.
Uncountablehow much / a little (alcohol, cotton)How much alcohol? A little cotton.
some / anyboth types of nounDo you have any gloves? We have some.
Payingin cash / by cardI will pay in cash.

Key point / Ponto-chave: se você pode contar (one glove, two gloves), use how many e a few; se não pode contar (alcohol, cotton, cash), use how much e a little.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"How gloves do you need?"
"How alcohol is left?"
"I need a masks."
"We have a cotton in stock."
"Is there a on a large order?"
"I will pay in and keep the receipt."

Stage 2: Put the Order Steps in Sequence

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of the order in the correct order.

?After that, I call the store and ask for the prices.
?First, I make a list of everything we need.
?Finally, I pay the cashier and keep the receipt for my records.
?Then I check how many boxes of gloves and how much alcohol are left.
?Next, I ask for a discount on the large quantity.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

How many boxes of gloves do you have?
Quantas caixas de luvas você tem?
How much alcohol is left in stock?
Quanto álcool resta em estoque?
Is there a discount on a large order?
Há um desconto em pedidos grandes?
I will pay in cash. Can I have the receipt?
Vou pagar em dinheiro. Pode me dar o recibo?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best phrase for each situation.

You want to know the number of masks the store has. What do you ask?
A "How much masks do you have?"
B "How many masks do you have?"
C "How long masks do you have?"
You need only a small amount of cotton. What do you say?
A "I need a little cotton."
B "I need a few cotton."
C "I need many cotton."
You want to pay with money and get proof of payment. What do you say?
A "I will pay by refund and skip the receipt."
B "I will pay with cash and lose the receipt."
C "I will pay in cash. Can I have the receipt?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are ordering supplies for your clinic this month. Out loud, ask about three items: use how many for countable things (gloves, masks) and how much for uncountable things (alcohol, cotton). Then say how you will pay and ask for a discount.

Survival Card -- Lesson 11

1How many boxes of gloves do you have?Quantas caixas de luvas você tem?
2How much alcohol is left in stock?Quanto álcool resta em estoque?
3Is there a discount on a large order?Há um desconto em pedidos grandes?
4I need a few masks and a little cotton.Preciso de algumas máscaras e um pouco de algodão.
5I will pay in cash. Can I have the receipt?Vou pagar em dinheiro. Pode me dar o recibo?
Aula 12 -- Pre-class

On the Phone

Schedule appointments on the phone with polite indirect questions. New vocabulary: caller, voicemail, reception, extension, dial, available, message, connect, hold on, hang up.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Caller -- the person who makes a phone call
"The caller wanted to book an appointment." (Quem liga)
Voicemail -- a recorded message you leave when nobody answers
"Please leave a message on the voicemail." (Correio de voz)
Reception -- the front desk that answers calls and greets people
"Call the reception to schedule a visit." (Recepção)
Extension -- an internal phone number inside an office
"Dial extension two zero one for the nurse." (Ramal)
Dial -- to press the numbers to make a phone call
"First, dial the number and wait." (Discar)
Available -- free and able to talk, help or meet
"The doctor is available at three o'clock." (Disponível)
Message -- the information you leave for someone to read or hear
"Can I leave a message for the doctor?" (Recado)
Connect -- to put a phone call through to another person
"I will connect you to the reception now." (Transferir)
Hold on -- to wait on the line for a moment
"Please hold on while I check." (Aguardar)
Hang up -- to end a phone call and put the phone down
"Do not hang up before you confirm the time." (Desligar)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Caller
Voicemail
Reception
Extension
Dial
Available
Message
Connect
Hold on
Hang up

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

Doctor Rubens calls the clinic to schedule a visit. He dials the number, and the reception answers. He says: "Rubens Tofolo speaking." Then he uses polite, indirect questions: "Could you tell me when the doctor is available? And do you know if I need an appointment?" The receptionist asks him to hold on, and then she gives him a time. Before he hangs up, Rubens repeats the date and time, so there is no mistake.

1. Which indirect question does Rubens use?
A Could you tell me when the doctor is available?
B Could you tell me when is the doctor available?
C Could you tell me when does the doctor available?
2. What does the receptionist ask him to do?
A To hang up and call again later.
B To hold on while she checks.
C To leave a voicemail message.
3. What does Rubens do before he hangs up?
A He asks for a discount.
B He repeats the date and time.
C He dials the wrong number.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- indirect questions

GRAMMAR

Perguntas indiretas são mais educadas. Depois de could you tell me usamos a ordem de uma frase afirmativa. / Indirect questions are more polite. After could you tell me we use statement word order.

Direct / DiretaIndirect / Indireta (polite)
When is the doctor available?Could you tell me when the doctor is available?
What time does the clinic open?Can you tell me what time the clinic opens?
Do I need an appointment?Do you know if I need an appointment?
Can you connect me?I was wondering if you could connect me.

Key point / Ponto-chave: depois de could you tell me / do you know / I was wondering if, use a ordem normal (sujeito + verbo), sem do/does; para perguntas de sim/não, use if ou whether.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Could you tell me when the doctor available?"
"Do you know I need an appointment?"
"Can you tell me what time the clinic ?"
"Please on while I check."
"Can I leave a for the doctor?"
"I will the number and wait for the reception."

Stage 2: Put the Call Steps in Sequence

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of the phone call in the correct order.

?After that, I ask my indirect questions politely, such as could you tell me when the doctor is available.
?First, I dial the number and wait for the reception to answer.
?Finally, I thank the person and hang up the phone.
?Then I say my name: good morning, Rubens Tofolo speaking.
?Next, I write down the date and time of the appointment.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

Good morning, Rubens Tofolo speaking.
Bom dia, aqui é o Rubens Tofolo.
Could you tell me when the doctor is available?
Você poderia me dizer quando o médico está disponível?
Do you know if I need an appointment?
Você sabe se eu preciso de um agendamento?
Can I leave a message on the voicemail?
Posso deixar um recado no correio de voz?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best phrase for each situation.

You want to ask politely when the doctor is free. What do you say?
A "When is the doctor available, tell me now?"
B "Could you tell me when the doctor is available?"
C "Could you tell me when is the doctor available?"
Nobody answers the phone at the clinic. What do you do?
A "I leave a message on the voicemail."
B "I hang up and never call again."
C "I dial the wrong number on purpose."
You answer the phone at your clinic. How do you say who you are?
A "I am the caller, hang up please."
B "This is reception, hold on the voicemail."
C "Good morning, Rubens Tofolo speaking."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are calling a clinic to schedule an appointment. Out loud, say who you are with "speaking", then ask two polite indirect questions (for example, when the doctor is available and if you need any documents). At the end, confirm the date and time before you hang up.

Survival Card -- Lesson 12

1Good morning, Rubens Tofolo speaking.Bom dia, aqui é o Rubens Tofolo.
2Could you tell me when the doctor is available?Você poderia me dizer quando o médico está disponível?
3Do you know if I need an appointment?Você sabe se eu preciso de um agendamento?
4Can I leave a message on the voicemail?Posso deixar um recado no correio de voz?
5Please hold on while I check the date and time.Por favor, aguarde enquanto eu verifico a data e o horário.
Aula 13 -- Pre-class

Weather & Small Talk

Make friendly small talk about the weather at a congress, using the present continuous. New vocabulary: weather, forecast, sunny, cloudy, rainy, windy, chilly, foggy, temperature, small talk.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. Tap Listen to hear it.

Weather -- the state of the air outside: sun, rain, wind, clouds
"The weather is lovely today." (Tempo / clima)
Forecast -- a report that says what the weather will be
"The forecast says it will be sunny." (Previsão do tempo)
Sunny -- with a lot of sun in the sky
"It is a bright, sunny morning." (Ensolarado)
Cloudy -- with many clouds and little sun
"The sky is grey and cloudy." (Nublado)
Rainy -- with a lot of rain
"Take an umbrella, it is a rainy day." (Chuvoso)
Windy -- with strong wind
"It is very windy near the river." (Ventoso)
Chilly -- a little cold, not warm
"It gets chilly in the evening." (Friozinho)
Foggy -- when there is fog and you cannot see far
"It is grey and foggy this morning." (Com neblina)
Temperature -- how hot or cold something is
"The temperature is twenty degrees." (Temperatura)
Small talk -- light, friendly conversation about easy topics
"We made small talk during the coffee break." (Conversa informal)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its translation.

Weather
Forecast
Sunny
Cloudy
Rainy
Windy
Chilly
Foggy
Temperature
Small talk

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Read the text and answer the questions.

It is the coffee break at the endocrinology congress in Lisbon. Outside, the sun is shining and a light wind is blowing from the ocean. Doctors are standing near the windows with coffee. Rubens is looking for someone to talk to. A colleague is waiting alone, so Rubens breaks the ice with the weather: "It is a beautiful day, isn't it? The forecast says it is getting warmer this afternoon." They are smiling now, and they are talking about the city. Small talk is working.

1. What is the weather doing during the coffee break?
A The sun is shining and a light wind is blowing.
B It is raining and it is very cold.
C It is snowing near the ocean.
2. How does Rubens break the ice?
A He talks about his medical research.
B He makes a friendly comment about the weather.
C He asks for the colleague's phone number.
3. According to the forecast, what is happening this afternoon?
A It is getting colder and windy.
B It is getting warmer.
C It is starting to rain.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- present continuous

GRAMMAR

Usamos o present continuous para ações que acontecem AGORA: be (am/is/are) + verbo com -ing. / We use the present continuous for actions happening NOW: be (am/is/are) + verb + -ing.

Form / FormaExample / Exemplo
Affirmative / AfirmativaThe sun is shining. / O sol está brilhando.
Negative / NegativaIt is not raining. / Não está chovendo.
Question / InterrogativaIs it getting chilly? / Está ficando friozinho?
People (plural)The doctors are arriving. / Os médicos estão chegando.

Key point / Ponto-chave: use am com I, is com he/she/it, are com you/we/they, sempre seguido do verbo terminado em -ing. Para o tempo, dizemos it is raining (não "it rains") quando falamos de agora.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct word. Tap Listen to hear the full sentence.

"Look outside, the sun shining."
"It is not raining, but it is getting ."
"The doctors are for the morning session."
"The says it will be sunny tomorrow."
"It is a great day to make small at the coffee break."
"The is twenty degrees this morning."

Stage 2: Put the Small Talk Steps in Sequence

Order

Listen first, then put the steps for starting small talk in the correct order.

?After that, I introduce myself and ask a light question about the congress.
?First, I find someone who is standing alone and I smile.
?Finally, I find something we have in common and we keep the conversation going.
?Then I break the ice with an easy comment about the weather.
?Next, I listen carefully and react to the answer.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it.

It is a beautiful day today, isn't it?
Está um dia lindo hoje, não está?
The sun is shining and the temperature is rising.
O sol está brilhando e a temperatura está subindo.
Is this your first time at the congress?
É a sua primeira vez no congresso?
The forecast says it is getting chilly this evening.
A previsão diz que vai ficar friozinho hoje à noite.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best phrase for each situation.

You meet a colleague at the coffee break and want to break the ice. What do you say?
A "What is your salary at your hospital?"
B "It is a lovely day today, isn't it?"
C "Goodbye, have a nice day."
You want to describe the weather right now (it is happening now). What do you say?
A "It is raining and the wind is blowing."
B "It rain and the wind blow."
C "It rained and the wind will blow."
A colleague is standing alone near the window. How do you start a friendly chat?
A "Please hold on while I check."
B "Could you tell me your extension?"
C "Hi, is this your first time at the congress?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question below. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you are at a coffee break during a congress. Out loud, describe the weather today using the present continuous (for example, the sun is shining or it is raining), then break the ice with a colleague: make a friendly comment about the weather and ask one light question about the conference.

Survival Card -- Lesson 13

1It is a beautiful day today, isn't it?Está um dia lindo hoje, não está?
2The sun is shining and the temperature is rising.O sol está brilhando e a temperatura está subindo.
3Is this your first time at the congress?É a sua primeira vez no congresso?
4The forecast says it is getting chilly this evening.A previsão diz que vai ficar friozinho hoje à noite.
5It was nice talking to you. Enjoy the conference.Foi bom conversar com você. Aproveite o congresso.

IN CLASS -- Selecione a aula

01
Who Is Rubens Today
Diagnostic Conversation and Reactivation Baseline -- 40 slides
02
Daily Routines & Clinic Life
Present Simple for routines and adverbs of frequency -- 40 slides
03
Talking About the Past
Past Simple regular and irregular verbs -- 40 slides
04
Life Experiences
Present Perfect with ever, never, already, yet -- 33 slides
05
Describing People & Places
Comparatives & superlatives -- 28 slides
06
Making Plans
Going to vs will -- planning a congress trip -- 28 slides
07
At the Restaurant
Ordering food politely -- would like, could I have -- 28 slides
08
Block 1 Review
Reflect on your progress -- present perfect, strengths and goals -- 28 slides
09
Healthy Habits
Give healthy-living advice -- should, must and shouldn't -- 28 slides
10
Giving Directions
Give and follow directions -- imperatives and prepositions of place -- 28 slides
11
Shopping & Services
Buy medical supplies -- how much/many and countable/uncountable nouns -- 28 slides
12
On the Phone
Schedule appointments on the phone -- phone expressions and indirect questions -- 28 slides
13
Weather & Small Talk
Make small talk about the weather at a congress -- present continuous and weather vocabulary -- 28 slides

Materiais Complementares
